STATUS DEFINITIONS

CRITICAL ≥ 4 flagged, OR (≥ 3 flagged AND ≥ 2 watch). Immediate escalation required.
HIGH 3 flagged, OR (2 flagged AND ≥ 3 watch). Active credit review needed.
AMBER 1-2 flagged, OR (0 flagged AND ≥ 4 watch). Elevated monitoring.
GREEN 0 flagged AND ≤ 3 watch. Standard monitoring cadence.
Status is computed deterministically after every run from the 15-flag framework. Full methodology in the Methodology tab.

TMT sector deep-dive. Data center construction is the macro demand signal; hyperscaler CapEx is the public-company supply commitment; revenue backlog is the contracted demand. Company tables follow.

Hyperscaler CapEx (Stacked, LTM)

Trailing-12-month CapEx for MSFT + GOOGL + AMZN + ORCL, last 12 quarters. Each bar sums the 4 quarters ending at the labeled calendar quarter, neutralizing staggered fiscal-year effects (MSFT June, ORCL May, GOOGL/AMZN December).

HYPERSCALER REVENUE BACKLOG (RPO) — DATA QUALITY INVESTIGATION IN PROGRESS
The RPO chart is temporarily stubbed pending resolution of an XBRL aggregation issue. Multiple time-horizon records per company per period are being summed instead of taking the reported total. Estimated true RPO across MSFT + GOOGL + ORCL is approximately $600-700Bn; previous chart displayed materially higher values due to this issue. Chart will return once SEC XBRL dimension filtering is implemented.

Refresh Schedule

The dashboard runs automatically Monday through Friday at 7:55 AM ET (set 5 minutes off the hour to avoid GitHub Actions cron congestion). Two refresh modes balance data freshness with API cost:

RefreshWhenWhat RefreshesWhat's Cached
WEEKLYFriday morning, or any day if cache is > 10 days stale, or manually triggeredAll Claude data (Batches A+B + second-pass), all market data, all SEC EDGAR, all macroWrites claude_cache.json for the week
DAILYMonday through ThursdayMarket data (yfinance), macro (FRED), data center construction, plus targeted Claude news refresh ONLY for amber/red namesReuses Claude ratings + news from last Friday for green names

Why this design: ratings change quarterly at most, news changes daily for stressed names but rarely for stable green names. Refreshing everything daily wastes API budget. The daily refresh keeps the dashboard fresh on metrics that matter daily (stock prices, macro, news for at-risk names) while preserving cache for stable items. Cost: roughly $60-80/month sustained, vs $150+ daily-weekly equivalent.

Manual trigger: any time, via the GitHub Actions tab. Defaults to auto mode (weekly on Friday, daily otherwise), with the option to force weekly or daily refresh. Manual weekly refreshes cost ~$5-7; daily refreshes cost ~$1.50.

Override files (ratings_override.json and news_override.json) are applied on every run regardless of refresh mode, so manually entered data appears immediately. These files allow manual injection of credit-relevant news or rating actions when automated sources miss something material.

Quick Reference

Status across every tab is driven by one source of truth: the 15-flag red flag framework. Each flag has explicit numerical thresholds. The combined count of FLAGGED + WATCH per name determines its tier.

TierTriggerAction
CRITICAL≥ 4 flagged, OR (≥ 3 flagged AND ≥ 2 watch)Escalate
HIGH3 flagged, OR (2 flagged AND ≥ 3 watch)Review
AMBER1-2 flagged, OR (0 flagged AND ≥ 4 watch)Watch
GREEN0 flagged AND ≤ 3 watchMonitor

Note on methodology change: prior versions of this dashboard used a separate concern_score (0-100 composite) from Claude. As of May 2026, status is driven purely by the 15-flag framework for consistency across all tabs and to align with auditable credit officer practice. See Red Flags Framework section below for the 15 flag definitions.

Status Definitions (detailed)

Each row's status badge and corresponding action button are computed after every run from the 15-flag count. Tiers are mutually exclusive: a name belongs to exactly one tier.

TierDescriptionThreshold
CRITICALImmediate credit committee attention required. Significant or compounding credit deterioration across multiple categories.4+ hard flags, OR 3+ hard + 2+ watch
HIGHActive credit review needed within current cycle. Pattern of credit stress emerging.3 hard flags, OR 2 hard + 3+ watch
AMBERElevated monitoring. Single material concern, or multiple early warnings.1-2 hard flags, OR 0 hard + 4+ watch
GREENStandard monitoring cadence. No or minimal credit stress signals.0 hard flags AND 0-3 watch flags

Financial Metric Definitions

All metrics are LTM (Last Twelve Months) unless explicitly labeled FY. All dollar figures in $Bn unless noted.

MetricDefinitionSource
Revenue (LTM)Sum of four most recent non-overlapping quarterly Revenue observations. For 20-F foreign filers, the latest annual filing.SEC EDGAR XBRL Revenues or fallback chain
Growth YoYCurrent LTM Revenue divided by prior-year LTM Revenue, minus one. Compares 4 most recent quarters against the 4 preceding non-overlapping quarters.Derived from Revenue history
EBITDABuilt bottom-up: Operating Income (from income statement) + Depreciation & Amortization (sourced from the cash flow statement, since the income statement often does not break out D&A separately). LTM basis.SEC EDGAR OperatingIncomeLoss + DepreciationDepletionAndAmortization (CFS)
EBITDA MarginEBITDA divided by Revenue, expressed as percent.Derived
FCF (Free Cash Flow)GAAP unlevered FCF: Cash from Operations (CFO) minus absolute value of Capital Expenditures. LTM basis. This is the standard credit-analyst definition; not adjusted for working capital or capitalized leases.SEC EDGAR NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities − PaymentsToAcquirePropertyPlantAndEquipment
CashCash & cash equivalents at most recent reporting date.SEC EDGAR CashAndCashEquivalentsAtCarryingValue
Long-Term DebtNon-current portion of total debt at most recent reporting date. Tag chain walks 15 candidates including capital lease obligations, senior notes, secured/unsecured debt, mortgages payable.SEC EDGAR fallback chain (15 candidate tags)
Short-Term DebtCurrent portion of long-term debt + commercial paper + short-term borrowings. Tag chain walks 8 candidates.SEC EDGAR fallback chain
Total DebtLong-Term Debt + Short-Term Debt. For names where SEC EDGAR returns no debt data (20-F filers, unusual tag structures), falls back to yfinance balance sheet.SEC EDGAR primary, yfinance fallback
Net DebtTotal Debt minus Cash.Derived
Net Leverage (ND/EBITDA)Net Debt divided by LTM EBITDA. The standard rating-agency leverage measure. Investment-grade typically below 3.0x. >5.0x is the Red Flag threshold. Shown as n/a when Net Debt is negative (cash exceeds debt) or EBITDA is zero/negative.Derived
Interest CoverageEBITDA divided by LTM Interest Expense.SEC EDGAR InterestExpense + derived

Market Data Definitions

MetricDefinitionSource
Market CapCurrent share price times shares outstanding. From yfinance fast_info.yfinance
EV (Enterprise Value)Market Cap + Total Debt - Cash. The theoretical takeover price: what an acquirer would pay equity holders plus assume in debt, less captured cash. Shown as n/a when any of the three inputs is missing.Derived
1D / 1M / YTD / 1Y %Total return percentage change from previous close, 22 trading days back, start of calendar year, and 252 trading days back respectively. Negative values shown in red, positive in green.yfinance
52W High / LowHighest and lowest adjusted close over trailing 252 trading days. Each cell shows the price plus a context % indicating how far current price is from that level: 52W High shows "% below high" (red if > 10% below), 52W Low shows "% above low" (green if > 20% above).yfinance

Data Source Tags

Each financials row is tagged with its source. Tags appear next to the company name on the Financials and TMT tabs.

  • SEC Pulled from SEC EDGAR XBRL company facts API. Authoritative, audited filings.
  • EST Sourced from Claude web search. Used only when SEC EDGAR data is unavailable. Less reliable.
  • YF Fallback for Total Debt from yfinance balance sheet when SEC EDGAR returns no debt fields. Used for non-SEC filers (Nissan, Imperial Brands) and some 20-F foreign filers.

Warning icon (⚠) next to a company name indicates SEC EDGAR returned a validation warning (stale period, unusual ratio, or missing field). Hover for details.

Financials Tab

Summary row shows LTM metrics. Click any row with a ▸ to expand historical detail showing the last 3 fiscal years plus LTM. Income Statement & Cash Flow plus Capital Structure tables with YoY and trend columns. Historical years are reconstructed from quarterly XBRL data by summing 4 non-overlapping quarters ending at each fiscal year-end.

TMT Tab

Sector deep-dive with four chart layers and five subsection tables, top to bottom:

  • US Data Center Construction: monthly dollar value of private data center construction put in place in the US. Sourced from the US Census Bureau Value of Construction Put in Place (VIP) report via Our World in Data. The number represents the dollar value of construction work performed during the month (materials installed, labor performed) on private-sector data center projects nationwide. Includes new construction, additions, alterations, and major replacements; excludes maintenance and government-owned projects. Values are inflation-adjusted to constant 2021 US dollars using BLS Producer Price Index for new office building construction. Reported in millions of dollars per month. Lagged approximately 6 weeks (the May release covers March data). Chart shows monthly level and month-over-month change. KPI tiles show latest value, YoY %, 3-month average MoM change, and 5-year cumulative growth. Why it matters for credit: this is the macro demand signal for the entire data center buildout cycle. Rising values mean more physical construction; flattening values would signal cycle deceleration before it shows in hyperscaler earnings.
  • Hyperscaler CapEx (Stacked, Quarterly): quarterly CapEx for Microsoft, Alphabet, Amazon, and Oracle going back up to 16 quarters. Bars synchronize on calendar quarter end. Public-company supply commitment, quarterly cadence to surface inflection points.
  • Hyperscaler Revenue Backlog (RPO): quarterly Remaining Performance Obligation for hyperscalers, last 8 quarters. Pulled from SEC EDGAR XBRL RevenueRemainingPerformanceObligation and RemainingPerformanceObligation tags. Contracted demand: revenue under signed contracts but not yet recognized. Forward demand signal. Amazon may be excluded from the chart because AWS RPO is not separately tagged in SEC XBRL filings; including their broader ContractWithCustomerLiability would mix Prime memberships and gift card balances with AWS commercial backlog. Names with no RPO disclosure are noted in the chart subtitle.
  • Five subsection tables: Hyperscalers, Data Center & Tower REITs, Telecom, Hardware & EMS, Software/Payments/Services. Filter pills (Red/Amber/Green) apply across all subsections.

How the three charts work together: data center construction shows what's being physically built. Hyperscaler CapEx shows the public-company portion of that spend. RPO shows the contracted demand that buildout is serving. Rising construction and CapEx with rising RPO is a healthy expansion. Rising construction and CapEx with flat or falling RPO is the classic overbuild signal.

Red Flags Framework (15 Flags)

Universal 15-flag framework, 11 deterministically computed each run, 4 pending future data sources. The heat map renders all 15 columns with pending flags shown as N/A so the framework is honest about coverage.

#FlagThresholdWatchSourceStatus
1Leverage Too HighND/EBITDA > 5.0x> 4.0xSEC EDGARComputed
2Leverage ClimbingND/EBITDA +1.0x YoY+0.5xSEC EDGAR historyComputed
3Coverage ThinEBITDA/Interest < 3.0x< 4.5xSEC EDGARComputed
4Burning CashFCF negative 2 quarters1 quarterSEC EDGAR historyComputed
5Wall of Maturities>20% of debt as current portion>10%SEC EDGAR (ST debt / Total debt)Computed
6Refi at Higher RatesAvg coupon to refi >150bps above current>75bpsBond coupon dataPending
7Revenue ShrinkingRev YoY < -5%< -2%SEC EDGARComputed
8Margin CompressionEBITDA margin -300bps YoY-150bpsSEC EDGAR historyComputed
9Stock CollapseYTD < -25%< -15%yfinanceComputed
10Rating Pressure2+ Negative outlooks1 negativeRatings + overrideComputed
11Bad News in FilingsTrigger phrases in key devWatch phrasesClaude synthesisComputed
12Liquidity SqueezeCash / ST Debt < 1.0x< 1.5xSEC EDGARComputed
13Going ConcernAuditor going concern qualificationSubstantial doubt language10-K auditor opinion textPending
14Insider SellingLarge insider sales 90d (>$10MM or >5% shares)>$5MMForm 4 filingsPending
15Geographic RiskMaterial unhedged sanctioned/conflict exposureSingle-country >25% revenue10-K risk factors textPending

Why 4 flags are pending: Flag 6 requires bond-level coupon data outside the SEC XBRL companyfacts schema. Flags 13, 15 require text-extraction from 10-K filings (auditor opinion section, risk factors section), which is a separate pipeline. Flag 14 requires parsing Form 4 insider transactions, which is a different SEC API endpoint. The architecture leaves these as N/A placeholders rather than guessing; we can wire them in future iterations using either a manual override file (for known cases) or dedicated text-extraction passes.

Sign-off tier mapping: 0 flags = Comfortable. 1-2 flags = Watch. 3-4 flags = Review. 5+ flags = Escalate. Watch states accumulate separately; 3+ watch states alone elevate to Watch tier.

Macro Indicators

HY OAS, IG OAS, 10Y UST, and 2Y UST in the header strip pull directly from FRED. Spreads converted from percent to basis points for the header tile. The Macro tab displays the same FRED observations in their native percent format with category groupings (Rates, Spreads, Inflation, Labor, Activity).

Data Sources

SourcePurposeCache TTL
SEC EDGARFinancial metrics for SEC filers6 days
yfinanceMarket data, stock returns, Total Debt fallbackNone (live every run)
FREDMacro indicators (19 series across rates, spreads, inflation, labor, activity)20 hours
Our World in DataUS data center construction spending (mirrors Census Bureau VIP + BLS PPI)24 hours
Anthropic ClaudeRatings, news, earnings dates, key developments, Top 3None (live every run)

Refresh Cadence

  • Workflow runs daily at 8:00 AM ET on weekdays (cron schedule). Can be triggered manually via the Actions tab.
  • Daily refresh: market data + commodities + indices (yfinance), ratings + news + Top 3 (Claude), FRED macro, data center construction (Our World in Data).
  • Weekly refresh: SEC EDGAR financials (TTL 6 days). Delete financials_cache.json to force a refresh.
  • Each run logs to runs.json with full diagnostics. Keep last 60 runs.
